rules_evaluated.evidence: line 5: 'Start by prompting the system' (Godin warmth + practical); line 38: 'the agent is part of the debugging loop' (Sutherland reframe). Bootstrap voice trio is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Creator name banned in student-facing body

rules_evaluated.evidence: line 36: "The habit is simple: prompt for diagnosis before repair." Reads as author-we ("we teach this habit") masked as universal truth.
rules_evaluated.fix: Recast to second-person: "The habit is simple: you prompt for diagnosis before repair." Or: "Start with diagnosis before repair—that's the habit."

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Belief reference by name not number
Lecture meta belongs below maintainer cut
rules_evaluated.evidence: Placement (line 42), Strategic role (lines 44-45), Voice target (line 46) all correctly located below <!-- maintainer --> at line 40.
Student-facing body uses imperative and second-person consistently. Maintainer blocks use third-person appropriately.
No reassurance-shaped sentences (no 'it's OK if', 'no penalty', 'fine way to').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 5 'Do not start by tracing everything by hand' is punchy and followed by action. No over-softening.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT student-facing body
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 5-7: "Do not start by tracing everything by hand. / Start by prompting the system." The negation-callout (agent-over-manual) is positioning-defense (why agents work better) leaking into the teaching beat. Diagnostic phrase match: 'not... do X' (negation-callout) paired with agent advocacy.
rules_evaluated.fix: Drop the negation-callout framing. Lead with pure practice: "Start by prompting the system to find the root cause. First, ask Claude whether the failure is mainly a source problem, a processing problem, or a boundary problem." The diagnostic practice earns itself without defending against the hand-tracing strawman.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: Terms 'root cause', 'source problem', 'processing problem', 'boundary problem' are earned immediately in the numbered list (lines 14-17) after their introduction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: Agent language is peer-flat: 'the agent tried to use' (line 17), 'the agent is part of' (line 38). No combative verbs (bark, order, command, whip, force).
Framing is forward ('recovery move after first multi-agent coordination') not corrective gap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: Root cause, source/processing/boundary problems are introduced in numbered list immediately after. No OOB (out-of-blue) terms.
No unearned tech jargon. Register is engineering-direct, appropriate for Bootstrap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro banned at every module
rules_evaluated.evidence: Not a module debrief; mini-lecture. Rule doesn't apply.
No em-dashes in body; short, simple sentences throughout.
Correct usage: 'the agent tried to use data' (line 17) — agent for multi-step action. No LLM/Claude confusion.
Fenced block (lines 12-20) contains no [BRACKETS] placeholders. Fully deterministic instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 11: 'Find the root cause of this bug.' Under 20 words; semantic summary.
Correct: lead-in (line 11), *(Claude Code)* label (line 9 in markdown structure), blank, fenced block (11-20).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No markdown shout in fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: No **bold** or *italic* inside fence. Fenced block uses numbered list (1./2./3.) and plain text.
Prompt uses critique framing: 'Tell me whether', 'Show me the failure chain'. Interrogative, not defensive.
Maintainer (line 44): 'The module's lesson is not multi-agent works; it is multi-agent systems fail at seams you can inspect and improve.' M3 unsettled-competence mood is correctly honored and named.
Mood is 'you don't yet know how to fix' (not-knowing) not 'you broke it' (shame). Invites agency (inspect, improve).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lead is 'Start by prompting the system' (the disciplined move: diagnostic). Not 'Agents get stuck' (failure).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sales-copy rules do not apply to student-facing curriculum
rules_evaluated.evidence: File is Bootstrap module content, not buyer-facing. Category exemption.

summary: REVISE. One blocking violation: lines 5-7 value-prop leak (rule 13 — positioning-defense against manual debugging leaks into teaching beat). One non-blocking author-we at line 36. Maintainer block is correctly placed. All prompt mechanics pass. All student-facing voice and jargon rules pass. Strategy mood-contract honored. File is otherwise ship-ready with the noted fix.
